_meta.columnNamingConvention: camelCase — all column names must be double-quoted in raw SQL (e.g. "invoiceNumber", "serviceId")
_meta.softDeletes: Most tables have a "deletedAt" column. Always add WHERE "deletedAt" IS NULL unless explicitly querying deleted records.
_meta.timestamps: All tables have "createdAt" and "updatedAt". Dates are stored in UTC; display in WIB (UTC+7).
_meta.currency: All monetary amounts are in IDR (Indonesian Rupiah), stored as DECIMAL(15,2).

tables.name: orders
tables.description: Core transaction table. Every payment attempt creates one order. Contains the full lifecycle of a transaction from creation to settlement. This is the primary table for almost all queries.

columns.name: invoiceNumber
columns.description: Human-readable invoice number from the ticketing system (e.g. INV-2024-001234). Use this to look up a specific order.

columns.name: invoiceData
columns.description: JSON blob with full invoice/booking details sent by the ticketing system. Contains passenger names, departure, destination, seat info, etc.

columns.name: ticketData
columns.description: JSON blob with issued ticket data after successful payment.

columns.name: serviceId
columns.description: FK → services.id. Which payment service (gateway + type + provider combination) was assigned to this order.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current order status. Values: 'unpaid' (created, awaiting payment), 'paid' (payment received), 'done' (confirmed/settled), 'refund' (refunded), 'cancel' (cancelled), 'expire' (expired without payment).

columns.name: orderDate
columns.description: When the order was initiated/submitted by the ticketing system.

columns.name: paymentDate
columns.description: When payment was confirmed by the payment gateway. NULL means payment has not been received yet.

columns.name: amount
columns.description: Gross payment amount in IDR (what the customer pays).

columns.name: serviceFee
columns.description: Service/transaction fee charged by the payment gateway in IDR.

columns.name: netAmount
columns.description: Net amount received after deducting service fees (amount - serviceFee).

columns.name: pgName
columns.description: Name of the payment gateway used (e.g. 'Xendit', 'DOKU', 'Finnet'). Denormalised from payment_gateways for quick access.

columns.name: paymentType
columns.description: Payment method type name (e.g. 'Virtual Account', 'Credit Card', 'QR Code'). Denormalised from payment_types.

columns.name: paymentProvider
columns.description: Bank or e-wallet used (e.g. 'BCA', 'BRI', 'BNI', 'OVO', 'DANA'). Denormalised from payment_providers.

columns.name: vaNumber
columns.description: Virtual Account number assigned to this order. Only populated for VA payment type orders.

columns.name: webhookPassStatus
columns.description: Status of the webhook notification sent to the ticketing system. Indicates if the notification was successfully delivered.

columns.name: clientId
columns.description: FK → clients.id. Which merchant/client submitted this order.

columns.name: orderFrom
columns.description: Source system identifier — where the order originated.

columns.name: ticketOffice
columns.description: Ticket office or channel code from the ticketing system.

columns.name: reconData
columns.description: Reconciliation data matched from the payment gateway settlement file.

columns.name: settledAt
columns.description: When the order was reconciled/settled with the payment gateway.

columns.name: createdAt
columns.description: When this record was created in the middleware.

columns.name: updatedAt
columns.description: When this record was last updated.

columns.name: deletedAt
columns.description: Soft delete timestamp. NULL = active record.

tables.usageNotes: Join with pg_responses to see the full payment event trail. Join with services → payment_gateways / payment_types / payment_providers for normalised gateway info. The denormalised columns pgName, paymentType, paymentProvider are faster for simple lookups.

tables.name: pg_responses
tables.description: Audit log of every event/callback received from payment gateways for an order. Each row is one webhook or API response event. Multiple rows per order are normal — they represent the full event trail (e.g. VA created, VA paid, confirmation sent).

columns.name: orderId
columns.description: FK → orders.id. Which order this event belongs to.

columns.name: responseCode
columns.description: Event type code. Examples: 'CCAuth' (credit card auth), 'CCToken' (card tokenisation), 'VAPaid' (VA payment received), 'QRPaid' (QR payment received), 'VACreated' (VA number assigned), 'ROWebhook' (recurring order webhook).

columns.name: responseData
columns.description: Full JSON payload from the payment gateway for this event. Contains raw gateway response including transaction IDs, bank references, amounts, timestamps, and error codes.

columns.name: ordercreatedat
columns.description: Partition/range key — timestamp of the parent order's creation. Used for time-range partitioning.

columns.name: updatedAt
columns.description: When this record was last updated.

tables.usageNotes: To investigate what happened to an order, query WHERE "orderId" = '<uuid>' ORDER BY "createdAt" ASC. The responseCode identifies the event type; responseData has the raw gateway payload. Multiple VAPaid entries would indicate duplicate payment callbacks.

tables.name: services
tables.description: Configuration table linking a payment gateway, payment type, and payment provider into a single named service. An order is assigned one service. Fee calculation rules are stored here.

columns.name: name
columns.description: Human-readable service name (e.g. 'Xendit BCA Virtual Account').

columns.name: feeType
columns.description: 'inclusive' = fee is included in amount; 'exclusive' = fee is added on top.

columns.name: feeCalculation
columns.description: Fee rules as JSON: {"percentage": 0.7, "fixed": 4000} means 0.7% + Rp 4,000 fixed.

columns.name: updatedAt
columns.description: When this service was last updated.

tables.name: payment_gateways
tables.description: Registered payment gateway providers (e.g. Xendit, DOKU, Finnet). Each has a weight for load balancing — higher weight = more traffic routed to it.

columns.name: credential
columns.description: AES-encrypted API credentials. Do not expose in query results.

columns.name: weight
columns.description: Load balancing weight. Higher = more orders routed here. Used to split traffic between gateways.

columns.name: percentageRange
columns.description: Calculated percentage range for weighted routing (e.g. '0-70' means handles 70% of traffic).

tables.name: payment_types
tables.description: Types of payment methods supported (e.g. Virtual Account, Credit Card, QR Code). Names stored in three languages.

columns.name: paymentTypeName
columns.description: English name (e.g. 'Virtual Account', 'Credit Card', 'QR Code'). Unique.

tables.name: payment_providers
tables.description: Banks and e-wallets available as payment channels (e.g. BCA, BRI, BNI, OVO, DANA, LinkAja). Each provider belongs to one or more payment types via services.

tables.name: notif_logs
tables.description: Log of webhook notifications sent from pg-middleware to the ticketing system after a payment event. Each row represents one delivery attempt. Used to check if the ticketing system was properly notified of a payment.

columns.name: orderId
columns.description: FK → orders.id. Which order this notification is for.

columns.name: payload
columns.description: The JSON payload that was sent to the ticketing system's webhook URL.

columns.name: response
columns.description: The HTTP response received from the ticketing system. A non-2xx response or empty means delivery failed.

columns.name: responseAt
columns.description: When a response was received. NULL may indicate a timeout or failure.

columns.name: ordercreatedat
columns.description: Partition key — the parent order's creation time.

columns.name: createdAt
columns.description: When this log entry was created.

tables.usageNotes: To check if a payment notification was delivered: query WHERE "orderId" = '<uuid>' and inspect the response column. A NULL response or HTTP error indicates the ticketing system was not notified.

tables.name: api_logs
tables.description: Raw API access log imported from the nginx access log. Each row is one HTTP request to the middleware API. Used for performance monitoring and debugging individual requests.

columns.name: url
columns.description: Request path (e.g. '/api/v1/payment/check-billing'). Used to filter by endpoint.

columns.name: responseTime
columns.description: Response time in milliseconds. Use for performance analysis.

columns.name: accessDate
columns.description: When this request was made. Use for time-range filtering.

columns.name: importDate
columns.description: When this log entry was imported from nginx into the database.

tables.usageNotes: This table can be very large. Always filter by accessDate range. To find performance issues, query AVG("responseTime") grouped by url and date. URL patterns for payment gateways: '/webhook/v1/xvapaid' (Xendit VA), '/webhook/v1/xqrpaid' (Xendit QR), '/webhook/v1/finnet' (Finnet), '/webhook/v1/dvapaid' (DOKU VA), '/webhook/v1/dqrpaid' (DOKU QR).

tables.name: api_log_calcs
tables.description: Pre-aggregated daily API statistics per endpoint. Computed from api_logs by pgmd-processor. Use this for gateway performance analysis instead of querying api_logs directly — it is much faster.

columns.name: url
columns.description: API endpoint path these stats are for.

columns.name: apiCountDaily
columns.description: Total number of requests to this endpoint on logDate.

columns.name: apiCountHoursDaily
columns.description: JSON array of hourly call counts [0..23]. Index = hour of day.

columns.name: apiResTime
columns.description: JSON object with response time stats: {"min": x, "avg": x, "max": x} in milliseconds.

tables.usageNotes: Preferred table for gateway performance queries. Filter by url to target specific endpoints/gateways, then use logDate range. Cast apiResTime to JSON and extract avg/min/max fields for response time analysis.

tables.name: clients
tables.description: Merchant accounts. Currently one active client (KCIC ticketing system). Stores API credentials and webhook configuration.

columns.name: webhookUrl
columns.description: URL where payment notifications are sent after successful payment.

tables.name: users
tables.description: Backoffice user accounts for the admin panel. Not related to end-customer/passenger accounts.

columns.name: password
columns.description: Bcrypt-hashed password. Never expose in query results.

tables.name: reports
tables.description: Generated reconciliation/settlement reports. Each row represents one report job triggered by the finance team, with the report data stored as JSON.

columns.name: reportType
columns.description: Type of report (e.g. reconciliation, settlement, finance).

tables.name: api_log_debugs
tables.description: Debug log of incoming API requests including their signature verification status. Used for diagnosing authentication/signature issues.

columns.name: signature
columns.description: Signature header provided by the caller.

columns.name: signatureStatus
columns.description: 'accepted' or 'rejected'. Use to find requests that failed signature validation.

commonQueryPatterns.lookUpOrder: SELECT o.*, s.name AS serviceName, pg."pgName" AS gatewayName, pt."paymentTypeName", pp."paymentProviderName" FROM orders o LEFT JOIN services s ON o."serviceId" = s.id LEFT JOIN payment_gateways pg ON s."paymentGatewayId" = pg.id LEFT JOIN payment_types pt ON s."paymentTypeId" = pt.id LEFT JOIN payment_providers pp ON s."paymentProviderId" = pp.id WHERE o."invoiceNumber" = $1 AND o."deletedAt" IS NULL LIMIT 1
commonQueryPatterns.orderEventTrail: SELECT "responseCode", "responseData", "createdAt" FROM pg_responses WHERE "orderId" = $1 AND "deletedAt" IS NULL ORDER BY "createdAt" ASC
commonQueryPatterns.orderNotifications: SELECT "payload", "response", "initAt", "responseAt" FROM notif_logs WHERE "orderId" = $1 AND "deletedAt" IS NULL ORDER BY "createdAt" ASC
commonQueryPatterns.gatewayPerformance: SELECT "logDate", "url", "apiCountDaily", "apiResTime" FROM api_log_calcs WHERE "logDate" BETWEEN $1 AND $2 ORDER BY "logDate" ASC
commonQueryPatterns.ordersByStatus: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM orders WHERE "deletedAt" IS NULL AND "createdAt" >= $1 GROUP BY status
commonQueryPatterns.revenueByGateway: SELECT "pgName", COUNT(*) AS orderCount, SUM(amount) AS totalAmount, SUM("netAmount") AS totalNet FROM orders WHERE status IN ('paid','done') AND "deletedAt" IS NULL AND "paymentDate" BETWEEN $1 AND $2 GROUP BY "pgName"
